title: 9 Herbal Creams For Hyperthyroidism
intro_desc: Herbal creams for hyperthyroidism work by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, which can help alleviate symptoms like anxiety and rapid heartbeat. One of the reasons herbal teas can relieve hyperthyroidism is by reducing the production of thyroid hormones. For example, Curcuma longa, or turmeric, contains a compound called curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce thyroid inflammation. Ginger, or Zingiber officinale, has a similar effect, and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to calm the digestive system and reduce anxiety. Another herb, Silybum marianum, or milk thistle, has been shown to reduce liver inflammation, which is often associated with hyperthyroidism.  When used in creams, these herbs can be absorbed directly into the skin, where they can work to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation. This can lead to a range of benefits, including reduced anxiety and depression, improved sleep quality, and a reduction in palpitations and tremors. By reducing the production of thyroid hormones, herbal creams can also help to lower your body temperature and heart rate, which can be especially beneficial for people who experience heat intolerance and rapid heartbeat.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains bioactive constituents like curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin. These compounds have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties that help regulate thyroid function. Curcumin, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the production of thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) and reduce the conversion of T4 to T3, which is often elevated in hyperthyroidism. The anti-inflammatory properties of these compounds may also help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism symptoms. By reducing inflammation and regulating thyroid hormone production, Curcuma longa creams may help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for hyperthyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of Curcuma longa powder.
Melt the coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of simmering water.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly. Add the Curcuma longa powder and mix well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely. Once cooled, use a hand mixer to whip the mixture until it becomes light and fluffy.
Apply the Curcuma longa cream to the affected area 2-3 times a day as needed, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains active constituents like gingerol, shogaol, and zingerone, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can be associated with hyperthyroidism symptoms. Gingerol and shogaol also have a regulatory effect on thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) levels, which can become imbalanced in hyperthyroidism. Additionally, zingerone has a potent antioxidant effect that can protect the thyroid gland from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and regulating TSH levels, Zingiber officinale creams may help alleviate some symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for hyperthyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of ginger root powder (Zingiber officinale), and 2 tablespoons of shea butter.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Add the ginger root powder and shea butter to the melted mixture. Stir well until combined.
Pour the mixture into a silicone mold or a small tin container. Let it cool and harden at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Once hardened, remove the cream from the mold and store it in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 2 weeks. Use as needed to help manage hyperthyroidism symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum creams contains silymarin, a compound made of silibinin, silidianin, and silicristin. Silymarin has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the liver and thyroid gland. It also has a regulatory effect on thyroid hormone production, which can help to decrease excessive thyroid hormone levels in hyperthyroidism. Silicristin, a key component of silymarin, has been shown to inhibit the conversion of T4 to T3, a process that can contribute to hyperthyroidism. By reducing T3 production, silymarin can help to alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as weight loss, rapid heartbeat, and anxiety.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal creams for hyperthyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Silybum marianum flowers and 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil).
Combine the dried flowers and carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Let it steep for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
After the steeping process, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean jar. Discard the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax to the oil mixture and heat it in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each heating, until the beeswax is fully melted.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool. Store the cream in a cool, dark place. Use as needed for hyperthyroidism relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin that has anti-inflammatory properties. It also contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are antioxidants that help protect the body from damage. Glycyrrhizin helps to block the conversion of cortisol to cortisone, reducing the level of cortisol in the body. Cortisol is a hormone that, when produced in excess, can contribute to hyperthyroidism symptoms, such as weight loss and rapid heartbeat. Glycyrrhizin's ability to reduce cortisol levels can help alleviate these symptoms and promote a more balanced thyroid function.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for hyperthyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of glycerin, 2 tablespoons of glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, 1 tablespoon of beeswax, and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil.
Combine 1/2 cup of distilled water and 2 tablespoons of glycyrrhiza glabra root powder in a bowl. Stir well to form a paste.
In a separate bowl, mix 1/2 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of glycerin, 1 tablespoon of beeswax, and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil. Heat the mixture gently until the beeswax melts.
Combine the glycyrrhiza glabra paste and the melted mixture in a blender. Blend until smooth.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool. Store the creams in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa creams contains a combination of active constituents, including avenanthramides, avenacosides, and beta-glucans. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the thyroid gland. Avenanthramides, in particular, have been shown to inhibit the production of thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH), a hormone that can contribute to hyperthyroidism. Beta-glucans, on the other hand, have been found to stimulate the production of cytokines, which can help regulate the immune system's response to thyroid hormones. By reducing inflammation and regulating hormone production, Avena sativa creams may provide relief for individuals experiencing symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
remedies.image_alt: Avena sativa herbal creams for hyperthyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Avena sativa (oat straw), 2 cups of coconut oil, and 1/2 cup of beeswax.
Combine the dried Avena sativa and 1 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler. Heat the mixture on low heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl. Discard the solids.
Add the remaining 1 cup of coconut oil and 1/2 cup of beeswax to the bowl. Mix well until the beeswax is melted.
Pour the mixture into a jar or tin. Let it cool and harden before use. Apply as needed to affected areas.

remedies.remedy_desc: I can't fulfill that request.  Echinacea purpurea creams are not a known treatment for hyperthyroidism.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for hyperthyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of lavender oil.
Combine 2 cups of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers with 2 cups of boiling water. Let it steep for 10 minutes. Strain the mixture and let it cool.
In a double boiler, melt 1 cup of coconut oil and 1/2 cup of beeswax. Stir until the mixture is smooth.
Add the cooled Echinacea mixture, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of lavender oil to the melted coconut oil mixture. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool. Once set, your Echinacea purpurea cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus creams contains a rich mixture of bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to calm the immune system and reduce thyroid inflammation. Astragalus membranaceus also contains immunomodulatory compounds like astragalosides, which help to regulate the thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) and prevent an overactive thyroid gland. The antioxidants present in the cream, such as ferulic acid and isorhapontigenin, help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, further contributing to thyroid health. By reducing inflammation, regulating TSH, and neutralizing free radicals, Astragalus membranaceus creams may help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
remedies.image_alt: Astragalus membranaceus herbal creams for hyperthyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Astragalus membranaceus root, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Astragalus membranaceus root to the melted oil and beeswax mixture. Let it steep for 2-3 hours, then strain the mixture.
Add the vitamin E oil to the strained mixture and stir well. Remove from heat and let it cool slightly.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely. Once cooled, the Astragalus membranaceus cream is ready for use. Apply 1-2 tablespoons as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera creams contain bioactive constituents like withanolides, alkaloids, and glycosides. These compounds have adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism. Withanolides, in particular, have been shown to inhibit the production of thyroid hormones, while glycosides help regulate the immune system and reduce inflammation. The alkaloids present in Withania somnifera creams have antioxidant properties that protect the body from oxidative stress caused by hyperthyroidism. By regulating the thyroid hormone production and reducing inflammation, Withania somnifera creams may help manage hyperthyroidism symptoms and promote overall well-being.
remedies.image_alt: Withania somnifera herbal creams for hyperthyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 100g dried Withania somnifera root, 1 cup coconut oil, 1/4 cup beeswax, 2 tablespoons shea butter, 2 tablespoons vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil, beeswax, and shea butter in a double boiler. Heat until beeswax dissolves.
Grind dried Withania somnifera root into a fine powder.
Add ground Withania somnifera powder and vitamin E oil to the melted mixture. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool. Once solid, store the cream in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng creams contains bioactive constituents like ginsenosides, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help reduce thyroid inflammation and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism. Ginsenosides also have antioxidant properties, which can protect the thyroid gland from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, Panax ginseng creams contain saponins, which can help regulate thyroid function and balance hormone levels. By reducing inflammation and regulating hormone levels, Panax ginseng creams may provide relief from hyperthyroidism symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Panax ginseng herbal creams for hyperthyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Panax ginseng extract, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the distilled water and Panax ginseng extract to the melted mixture. Stir well until the extract is fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly. Stir in the vitamin E oil.
Pour the mixture into a clean, sterilized container and let it cool and solidify completely before using.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent hyperthyroidism from forming. Some herbal teas, like those made from ashwagandha and bladderwrack, have properties that can help regulate thyroid function. These teas may also help reduce stress and inflammation, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism. However, more research is needed to fully understand the effects of herbal tea on thyroid health.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas for hyperthyroidism every day can be a bit tricky. While some teas, like peppermint and chamomile, are generally safe, others like dandelion and licorice root may interfere with medication or worsen symptoms. It's best to limit or avoid them until you talk to your doctor about using herbal teas as a supplement.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas may help manage hyperthyroidism symptoms, but the time it takes to show results varies. Some people notice improvements within a few days to a week, while others may not see a difference for several weeks. It's essential to be consistent with herbal tea consumption and monitor symptoms closely.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for hyperthyroidism can help calm your body and mind. It's best to have it in the evening, before bed, when your body is preparing for rest. This allows the soothing properties of the tea to take effect, promoting relaxation and a good night's sleep.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can potentially interact with prescription medications for hyperthyroidism. Some herbs, like licorice root and passionflower, may slow down or speed up the body's ability to process thyroid hormones. This can affect how well the medications work. For example, licorice root may decrease the effectiveness of certain medications. It's essential to be aware of these interactions to ensure safe treatment.
faq_pregnant: It's generally not recommended to drink herbal teas for hyperthyroidism while pregnant. Some herbs can affect the thyroid gland or interact with medications, potentially harming the unborn baby. If you're experiencing hyperthyroidism symptoms during pregnancy, your body may need specialized care to ensure your health and the baby's.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding with hyperthyroidism, it's essential to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbs, like motherwort and licorice root, can mimic or worsen thyroid issues. Bladderwrack and kelp may contain iodine, which can affect thyroid function and milk production. However, some herbs like chamomile and peppermint are often considered safe during breastfeeding. Always check the ingredients and consult with a trusted source.
title_2: 9 Effective Herbal Creams for Women Suffering from Hyperthyroidism
